55	func (f *fakeExecuteBeadGit) HeadRev(dir string) (string, error) {
56		f.mu.Lock()
57		defer f.mu.Unlock()
58		if strings.Contains(dir, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix) {
59			if f.wtHeadRevErr != nil {
60				return "", f.wtHeadRevErr
61			}
62			return f.wtHeadRev, nil
63		}
64		if len(f.headRevSeq) > 0 {
65			idx := f.headRevIdx
66			if idx >= len(f.headRevSeq) {
67				idx = len(f.headRevSeq) - 1
68			}
69			rev := f.headRevSeq[idx]
70			f.headRevIdx++
71			return rev, nil
72		}
73		return f.mainHeadRev, nil
74	}

207	// fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit returns a LandingAdvancer callback that maps the
208	// fake git's Merge/UpdateRef semantics onto the coordinator-pattern advancer
209	// interface. Semantically, "mergeCalls" now means "number of times
210	// LandBeadResult invoked the advancer (attempted to advance the target
211	// branch)". Used by tests that were written against the old Merge() path so
212	// they continue to pass after the land-coordinator refactor.
213	func fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git *fakeExecuteBeadGit) func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
214		return func(res *agent.ExecuteBeadResult) (*agent.LandResult, error) {
215			if err := git.Merge("", res.ResultRev); err != nil {
216				preserveRef := agent.PreserveRef(res.BeadID, res.BaseRev)
217				// Record the preserve ref in the fake's refs map so tests that
218				// assert git.refs[preserveRef] continue to pass.
219				_ = git.UpdateRef("", preserveRef, res.ResultRev)
220				return &agent.LandResult{
221					Status:      "preserved",
222					PreserveRef: preserveRef,
223					Reason:      "merge failed",
224				}, nil
225			}
226			return &agent.LandResult{Status: "landed", NewTip: res.ResultRev}, nil
227		}
228	}

253	func parseExecuteBeadJSON(t *testing.T, out string) agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
254		t.Helper()

293	func copyTree(src, dst string) error {
294		return filepath.Walk(src, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, err error) error {
295			if err != nil {
296				return err
297			}
298			rel, err := filepath.Rel(src, path)
299			if err != nil {
300				return err
301			}
302			target := filepath.Join(dst, rel)
303			if info.IsDir() {
304				return os.MkdirAll(target, info.Mode())
305			}
306			if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(target), 0o755); err != nil {
307				return err
308			}
309			in, err := os.Open(path)
310			if err != nil {
311				return err
312			}
313			defer in.Close()
314			out, err := os.Create(target)
315			if err != nil {
316				return err
317			}
318			if _, err := io.Copy(out, in); err != nil {
319				out.Close()
320				return err
321			}
322			if err := out.Close(); err != nil {
323				return err
324			}
325			return os.Chmod(target, info.Mode())
326		})
327	}

356	// TestExecuteBeadMerge verifies that when the agent produces a real commit and
357	// the target branch can advance, the outcome is "merged". Exercises real git
358	// plumbing (worktree add/remove, ref update, file sync) and the script harness
359	// driving an actual commit — no fakes for the components under test.
360	func TestExecuteBeadMerge(t *testing.T) {
361		workDir := t.TempDir()
362	
363		scrubEnv := func() []string {
364			parent := os.Environ()
365			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
366			for _, kv := range parent {
367				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
368					continue
369				}
370				env = append(env, kv)
371			}
372			return env
373		}
374		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
375			t.Helper()
376			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
377			cmd.Dir = workDir
378			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
379			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
380			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
381			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
382		}
383	
384		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
385		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
386		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
387		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
388		runGit("add", ".")
389		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
390	
391		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
392			ID:        "my-bead",
393			Title:     "Test execute-bead merge",
394			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
395			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
396		})
397		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
398		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
399		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
400	
401		// Directive file drives the script harness to make one real commit in the
402		// worker worktree, replacing fakeAgentRunner's canned Result struct.
403		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
404		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
405			"create-file hello.txt hello-content\n"+
406				"commit feat: add hello\n",
407		), 0o644))
408	
409		// Real runner + real git ops: no overrides that fake the thing under test.
410		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
411		f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
412	
413		root := f.NewRootCommand()
414		out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "my-bead", "--json",
415			"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
416		require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
417		res := parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
418	
419		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
420		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
421		assert.Equal(t, baseSHA, res.BaseRev)
422		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.ResultRev)
423		assert.NotEqual(t, res.BaseRev, res.ResultRev)
424		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
425		assert.Equal(t, "my-bead", res.BeadID)
426		assert.NotEmpty(t, res.SessionID)
427	
428		// Real-git assertion: main must have advanced to ResultRev via Land().
429		assert.Equal(t, res.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD"),
430			"main should advance to ResultRev after a successful merge")
431	
432		// The worker worktree must have been created and cleaned up: no entries
433		// under the execute-bead prefix remain in `git worktree list`.
434		wtList := runGit("worktree", "list", "--porcelain")
435		assert.NotContains(t, wtList, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"my-bead-",
436			"execute-bead worktree should be removed after the run")

438		// File from the agent's commit must be materialized on main through real
439		// merge/ff semantics (regression guard for ddx-eaebaffb: update-ref
440		// bypassing the working tree).
441		content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"))
442		require.NoError(t, err, "hello.txt should materialize on main after Land()")
443		assert.Equal(t, "hello-content", string(content))
444	}
